title: [test] CLI help output and blank-input validation
description:
Integration test for the CLI help system and input validation.

FILE TO CREATE:
- tests/cli-help-validation.test.ts (new)

APPROACH (Jest):
- Use the in-process runCli(argv) helper (preferred) or child process to capture stdout, stderr, and exit code.

WHAT TO TEST (assert pass/fail):
Help:
- `--help` and `-h` exit 0 and stdout lists all five subcommands (list, read, create, update, delete).
- `create --help` exits 0 and mentions --title and --content.
Validation:
- `create --title "   " --content "x"` exits non-zero and stderr contains a clear error message.
- `create --content "x"` (missing required --title) exits non-zero with a clear message.
- `read` with no --id exits non-zero with a clear message.
No stack traces:
- For every error case above, assert the output does NOT contain "Error:" stack frames / "at " trace lines (no raw stack trace leaked).

DONE CRITERIA:
- `npm test` runs this suite and all assertions pass.
- Depends on the help impl task and the validation impl task.
notes: cost-estimate: bucket=S model=claude-sonnet-4-6 doer_tokens=600 reviewer_tokens=264 output_usd=NaN

title: [impl] Implement global and per-subcommand --help/-h
description:
Implement the CLI help system: global and per-subcommand --help/-h.

FILES TO CHANGE:
- src/cli/index.ts: detect --help/-h globally and per subcommand; dispatch to a help printer.
- src/cli/help.ts (new): export usage strings — a global usage (lists all subcommands: list, read, create, update, delete, plus global flags --help/-h, --version/-v) and a per-subcommand usage map.

BEHAVIOUR:
- `cli --help` and `cli -h` print global usage to stdout and exit 0.
- `cli <subcommand> --help` (and -h) print that subcommand's usage (its required/optional flags) and exit 0.
- `cli` with no args prints global usage (exit 0 acceptable) — do not crash.
- No stack traces anywhere.

DONE CRITERIA:
- `cli --help` exits 0 and output lists all five subcommands and the --version flag.
- `cli create --help` exits 0 and documents --title and --content as required.
- Compiles, lints clean, no `any`. Depends on the CLI scaffold and the subcommands existing so usage matches real commands.
notes: cost-estimate: bucket=M model=claude-sonnet-4-6 doer_tokens=1400 reviewer_tokens=616 output_usd=NaN

title: [test] CLI CRUD commands end-to-end against NoteAPI
description:
Integration test for the full CLI CRUD command set against a running NoteAPI.

FILE TO CREATE:
- tests/cli-crud.test.ts (new)

ENVIRONMENT (per integ-test-playbook.md):
- Start the NoteAPI in-process or on port 3001. Simplest robust approach: import the Express `app` from src/app and start it on an ephemeral port in beforeAll (app.listen(0)), set API_BASE_URL to that port, and call the CLI via an in-process runCli(argv) helper (preferred) or child process. Reset state with noteStore.clear() in beforeEach.

WHAT TO TEST (assert pass/fail via Jest):
- create: `create --title "T" --content "C" --tag work` exits 0 and output contains the new id/title.
- list: `list` returns the created note; `list --tag work` filters to it; `list --tag none` returns empty; `list --q T` finds it.
- read: `read --id <id>` prints the note (exit 0); `read --id missing` exits non-zero.
- update: `update --id <id> --title "T2"` exits 0 and reflects new title.
- delete: `delete --id <id>` exits 0; a subsequent `read --id <id>` exits non-zero (gone).
- API error path: any command against a non-existent id exits non-zero with NO stack trace in output.

DONE CRITERIA:
- `npm test` runs this suite and all assertions pass.
- Depends on all three mi2 impl tasks (client, list/read, create/update/delete).
notes: cost-estimate: bucket=M model=claude-sonnet-4-6 doer_tokens=1400 reviewer_tokens=616 output_usd=NaN

title: [impl] Implement CLI create, update, delete subcommands
description:
Implement the write-path CLI subcommands: `create`, `update`, `delete`.

FILES TO CHANGE:
- src/cli/index.ts: register and dispatch `create`, `update`, `delete`.
- src/cli/commands/create.ts, update.ts, delete.ts (new) (or extend the commands module), calling apiClient.

BEHAVIOUR:
- `create --title <t> --content <c>` (both REQUIRED, optional --tag repeatable for tags): POST /api/notes, print created note, exit 0.
- `update --id <id>` (REQUIRED) with optional --title/--content: PUT /api/notes/:id, print updated note, exit 0. 404 -> clear error, non-zero exit.
- `delete --id <id>` (REQUIRED): DELETE /api/notes/:id, print confirmation, exit 0. 404 -> clear error, non-zero exit.
- Missing required flags -> clear validation error to stderr, non-zero exit, no stack trace.

DONE CRITERIA:
- All three commands work against a running server on port 3001 (create then update then delete round-trip).
- Required-flag omission yields non-zero exit with a clear message.
- Compiles, lints clean, no `any`. Depends on the API client module.
notes: cost-estimate: bucket=L model=claude-sonnet-4-6 doer_tokens=2800 reviewer_tokens=1232 output_usd=NaN

title: [impl] Implement CLI list and read subcommands
description:
Implement the read-path CLI subcommands: `list` and `read`.

FILES TO CHANGE:
- src/cli/index.ts: register and dispatch `list` and `read` subcommands.
- src/cli/commands/list.ts (new) and src/cli/commands/read.ts (new) (or a single commands module if simpler), calling apiClient.

BEHAVIOUR:
- `list`: optional --tag <tag> and --q <query> filters passed through to GET /api/notes. Prints the returned notes to stdout (human-readable). Exit 0 on success.
- `read --id <id>`: --id is REQUIRED; prints the single note. If the note is missing (404), print a clear error to stderr and exit non-zero (e.g. exit 1). Missing --id prints a usage/validation error and exits non-zero.
- On any API error, print "{ error: message }"-style human-readable message (no stack trace) and exit non-zero.

DONE CRITERIA:
- `cli list` and `cli list --tag work` and `cli list --q meeting` work against a running server on port 3001.
- `cli read --id <existing>` prints the note; `cli read --id missing` exits non-zero with a clear message.
- Compiles, lints clean, no `any`. Depends on the API client module.
notes: cost-estimate: bucket=M model=claude-sonnet-4-6 doer_tokens=1400 reviewer_tokens=616 output_usd=NaN

title: [impl] Add NoteAPI HTTP client module for the CLI
description:
Create an HTTP API client module the CLI uses to talk to the NoteAPI.

FILES TO CREATE/CHANGE:
- src/cli/apiClient.ts (new): functions listNotes({tag?, q?}), getNote(id), createNote({title, content, tags?}), updateNote(id, {title?, content?}), deleteNote(id). Uses global fetch (Node 18+). Base URL from env API_BASE_URL, default "http://localhost:3001/api/notes" (matches integ-test-playbook port 3001).
- Define a typed ApiError (or reuse src/models) so non-2xx responses throw an error carrying the HTTP status and the server's { error } / { errors } message. No `any` types — type responses against the Note interface from src/models/note.ts.

BEHAVIOUR:
- Each function returns parsed JSON (or void for delete which returns 204).
- Non-2xx responses reject with an ApiError exposing status and a human-readable message (never a raw response object or stack trace).

DONE CRITERIA:
- Module compiles under `npm run build` with no `any` and passes `npm run lint`.
- Functions cover all five operations (list, get, create, update, delete) with correct HTTP methods and query params.
- Depends on the CLI scaffold (entrypoint) existing.

title: [test] --version/-v flag prints version and exits 0
description:
Add an integration test verifying the --version / -v flag of the CLI.

FILE TO CREATE:
- tests/cli-version.test.ts (new)

HOW TO TEST (Jest):
- Invoke the built CLI as a child process using node/ts-node, e.g. execFileSync("npx", ["ts-node", "src/cli/index.ts", "--version"]) capturing stdout and exit code. Alternatively import a runCli(argv) function from src/cli that returns {stdout, exitCode} without calling process.exit, and assert on it. Prefer the in-process runCli approach if the impl exposes one (cleaner, faster).

ASSERTIONS (pass/fail):
- stdout trimmed equals "fleet-e2e-toy v1.0.0".
- exit code is 0.
- "-v" produces identical output and exit code 0.
- "--version list create" (version combined with other args) still prints the version and exits 0.

DONE CRITERIA:
- `npm test` runs this suite and all assertions pass.
notes: cost-estimate: bucket=S model=claude-sonnet-4-6 doer_tokens=600 reviewer_tokens=264 output_usd=NaN

title: [impl] Scaffold CLI entrypoint and --version/-v flag
description:
Scaffold a Node+TypeScript CLI entrypoint and implement the --version / -v flag.

FILES TO CREATE/CHANGE:
- src/cli/index.ts (new): main CLI entrypoint. Parse argv, dispatch to subcommands (stub for now), handle global flags.
- src/cli/version.ts (new): export VERSION constant "fleet-e2e-toy v1.0.0" (single source of truth).
- package.json: add a "cli" script: "ts-node src/cli/index.ts" so the CLI can be invoked.

BEHAVIOUR:
- Running the CLI with --version or -v prints exactly "fleet-e2e-toy v1.0.0" to stdout and exits with code 0.
- The flag works even when combined with other args (e.g. "--version list" still prints version and exits 0; --version takes precedence).
- No stack traces. Follow project conventions: no `any` types, no console.log inside shared logic except the CLI's own stdout writes (CLI output is allowed to use process.stdout/console.log at the top level since these are not Express route handlers).

DONE CRITERIA:
- `npx ts-node src/cli/index.ts --version` prints "fleet-e2e-toy v1.0.0" and exit code is 0.
- `-v` behaves identically.
- TypeScript compiles (`npm run build`) with no errors and `npm run lint` passes.

title: CLI CRUD commands (list/read/create/update/delete)
description: Implement five subcommands against the NoteAPI: list (optional --tag/--q filter), read (--id required), create (--title, --content required), update (--id required, --title/--content optional), delete (--id required). Each command calls the API and prints results to stdout. Non-zero exit on API error.

title: CLI help system and input validation
description: The CLI must support --help/-h flags globally and per subcommand, printing usage and exiting 0. Input validation must reject empty or whitespace-only arguments with a clear error message and non-zero exit code. No stack traces in error output.

title: [impl] CLI validation: reject empty/blank argument values
description:
Add input validation to the CLI that rejects empty or whitespace-only argument values.

FILES TO CHANGE:
- src/cli/validation.ts (new): a helper (e.g. requireNonBlank(name, value)) that throws a CliValidationError when a provided flag value is empty or whitespace-only.
- src/cli/index.ts (or the command modules): apply the helper to user-supplied flag values (--title, --content, --id, --tag, --q) before calling the API.

BEHAVIOUR:
- Passing an empty string or whitespace-only string for a required flag (e.g. `create --title "   " --content "x"`) prints a clear, human-readable error message to stderr and exits with a non-zero code.
- No stack trace is shown for validation failures.
- Missing required flags are also reported clearly (may reuse this path).

DONE CRITERIA:
- `create --title "" --content "x"` exits non-zero with a clear message and no stack trace.
- A unit/integration test (added in the 7rp test task) exercises blank-string rejection.
- Compiles, lints clean, no `any`. Depends on the subcommands and CLI scaffold.

title: Add --version flag to CLI
description: The CLI tool should support a --version (or -v) flag that prints the current version string and exits with code 0. Acceptance: running ./tool --version prints fleet-e2e-toy v1.0.0, exit code 0, works alongside other flags.

title: Add config file support (~/.fleet-e2e-toy.yaml)
description: Allow users to persist default flag values in a YAML config file at ~/.fleet-e2e-toy.yaml so they don't have to repeat common flags on every run. Acceptance: reads config on startup, CLI flags override config, warns on unknown keys, documents supported keys in README.

title: Handle SIGINT gracefully (Ctrl-C)
description: The tool should catch SIGINT (Ctrl-C) and exit cleanly without a Python traceback or incomplete output. Acceptance: Ctrl-C prints 'Interrupted.' and exits with code 130, no stack trace shown, partial output files cleaned up.

title: Add JSON output mode via --json flag
description: Add a --json flag so all command output can be emitted as machine-readable JSON for scripting and CI pipelines. Acceptance: --json flag accepted on any subcommand, output is valid JSON, human-readable output is default, errors also JSON-formatted.

title: Tag filtering endpoint
description: GET /api/notes?tag=work returns only notes with that tag. Already partially implemented - needs tests.
